package: Goal 29: Continuity From Static-Patch Modular/KMS Structure

scope.setting: finite fuzzy-sphere/static-patch modular flow, finite KMS/detailed-balance Schur channels, and modular-time averaging measures
scope.result_type: KMS detailed balance alone is insufficient; KMS plus modular-time approximate identity is sufficient for the Goal 28 continuity axiom
scope.bounded_family: 1 <= L <= 5, four selected modular/KMS models, perturbation radius 0.05
scope.claim_boundary: finite modular/KMS regulator selection only; not continuum de Sitter quantum gravity, dS/CFT, or literal ER=EPR in de Sitter

core_claim: Finite KMS/detailed-balance modular structure does not by itself force vanishing cutoff-continuity, but localized modular-time averages forming an approximate identity do; the selected models preserve the screen-shadow no-go and M_N versus C^N bridge distinction.

exact_finite_theorem_style_claims.claim: A modular-time average Phi_L(A)=int sigma_t(A)dmu_L(t) gives an energy-difference Schur channel with coefficients hat(mu_L)(E_i-E_j).
For symmetric modular-time measures, the Schur coefficients are real and even, so the finite channel is self-adjoint in the Gibbs/KMS inner product.
The stationary modular twirl is CPTP, diagonal preserving, and KMS detailed balanced, but it is complete dephasing and fails cutoff continuity.
If the modular-time measures converge to delta_0 uniformly on bounded cutoff energy gaps, the cutoff error vanishes and off-diagonal response survives.
For the selected finite models, screen shadows collide while off-diagonal response separates M_N from C^N.

obstruction_table.lesson: KMS detailed balance and diagonal preservation alone permit complete dephasing.
Detailed balance can hold while modular-time width fails to shrink with cutoff.
Thermal weighting is not enough without a normalized CPTP/unital time-average channel.

bounded_certificate_evidence.claim: Check cutoffs 1 through 5 for all selected modular/KMS models and perturbation variants.
Run focused tests for KMS insufficiency, approximate-identity sufficiency, detailed balance, bridge diagnostics, and input validation.

expert_feedback_summary: Goal 29 refines the Goal 28 continuity axiom. KMS/detailed balance alone is insufficient because stationary modular twirling gives total dephasing. KMS plus modular-time approximate identity is sufficient for the finite regulator class and preserves the screen-shadow no-go and M_N versus C^N bridge distinction.
limitations: The result is finite and modular/KMS-axiomatic. It does not derive modular-time approximate identity from continuum de Sitter gravity, observer modular theory, a path integral, or dS/CFT.
